_readme: Per-layer map alignment. The Map tab draws every tile layer onto the world rectangle [0,worldSize] with one shared transform, so a layer is only accurate if its source image spans exactly that rectangle (true for the heightmap-derived 'terrain' layer, not for higher-res rips). This file supplies a per-layer AFFINE that maps a layer's assumed-world position to its true world position. Absent layer / identity = no change (today's behaviour). Shape: { "<map>": { "<layerName>": { "affine": {a,b,c,d,e,f}, "points": [{assumed:[x,z], world:[x,z]}], "rms": <m>, "updated": "ISO" } } }. AUTHORED by the Map tab's Calibrate mode: mark control points, solve, preview live, then paste the emitted block here and deploy ConfigViewer. This file is COMMITTED and small - the tile manifest is not (it lives in gitignored web/tiles/ and is regenerated by MapDataExtraction), which is why calibration lives here and is overlaid at load, not written into the manifest.
